title: olea brachiata
intro: Olea brachiata, also known as the wight's olive or brachiata, is a flowering plant species native to the Western Ghats in India. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Olea brachiata include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and insecticide. The herbal preparations of Olea brachiata include its use in traditional Ayurvedic medicine, where it is often made into a paste or oil for topical application.
